pattern.title: Consonant gradation: kauppa → kaupas, katu → kadul
pattern.summary: Finnish rewrites its own middles when an ending arrives: kauppa → kaupassa, katu → kadulla, Helsinki → Helsingissä, leipä → leivän. The k, p or t weakens, which is why a word you know can be unrecognisable with a case on it. For everyday words like these, the plain form and the partitive are strong, while -n, -ssa and -lla pull the weak grade. A smaller group runs in reverse: -e, -as and -in words are weak plain and strengthen instead (osoite → osoitteen, opas → oppaan).
examples: Mä oon kaupas. = I'm at the shop. (kauppa → kaupas)
Se asuu Helsingis. = They live in Helsinki. (Helsinki → Helsingis)
Mä ostin leivän. = I bought the bread. (leipä → leivän)

glosses.jalka: leg / foot - strong grade here, in the plain word

glosses.jalkaan: into/at the leg - the -an ending keeps the strong grade, so the k stays

glosses.jalan: the leg - and here the k vanishes completely: jalka → jalan. Same word as two sentences ago

sentences.en: We went to the beach in the evening.

glosses.illal: in the evening - ilta → illalla, lt becomes ll
glosses.rannalle: to the beach - ranta → rannalle, nt becomes nn

glosses.tytön: girl's - tyttö → tytön, the double tt thins to t

sentences.fi: Mä ostin leipää ja pari omenaa.
sentences.en: I bought bread and a couple of apples.
sentences.written: Minä ostin leipää ja pari omenaa.

glosses.leipää: bread - partitive, and the partitive keeps the STRONG grade, so it's p here and not the v of leivän
glosses.omenaa: apple - partitive singular after a quantity word

sentences.en: I left the bread at the shop.

glosses.leivän: the bread - and now it's weak: leipä → leivän, p becomes v
glosses.kauppaan: to the shop - going TO restores the strong grade, so kauppa keeps its pp

sentences.en: The bank is on the other side of the street.

glosses.pankki: bank - strong in the plain word
glosses.kadun: of the street - katu → kadun, t becomes d

sentences.en: I'll go to the bank tomorrow.

glosses.pankis: at the bank - pankki → pankissa, kk thins to k, then -ssa clips to -s in speech

sentences.fi: Toi kenkä on rikki, kengän pohja irtos.
sentences.en: That shoe is broken, the sole came off.
sentences.written: Tuo kenkä on rikki, kengän pohja irtosi.

glosses.kengän: the shoe's - kenkä → kengän, nk becomes ng. Same sound change as Helsinki → Helsingissä
